user_request:
I want to create a table tracking AI companies that are actively hiring.

Columns needed:
- Company Name
- Website
- Is hiring for AI roles? (yes/no)
- Team size (approximate)
- Recent funding (last round)

Find about 10 companies across different AI sectors like research, healthcare, and enterprise.
Make sure to include both established companies and startups.

columns.name: entity_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for each entity in the table
columns.validation_strategy: Assign a unique alphanumeric ID to each entity upon entry; cross-check for duplicates before finalizing the table

columns.name: entity_name
columns.description: Official name of the organization, company, or research group
columns.validation_strategy: Verify name on official website, Crunchbase, LinkedIn, or reputable industry directories; cross-reference with press releases or news articles for consistency

columns.name: domain
columns.description: Primary industry or research focus (e.g., Healthcare, Enterprise, Academic)
columns.validation_strategy: Extract from 'About Us' or 'What We Do' sections on official websites; confirm with industry reports or Crunchbase categorization

columns.name: founding_year
columns.description: Year the entity was founded or established
columns.validation_strategy: Check Crunchbase, official website history/FAQ, LinkedIn company page, or press archives for founding announcements

columns.name: headquarters_location
columns.description: City and country of primary headquarters
columns.validation_strategy: Confirm on official website contact or 'About' page; cross-check with Crunchbase, LinkedIn, or Google Maps business listing

columns.name: team_size
columns.description: Approximate number of employees or members
columns.validation_strategy: Look for team size on LinkedIn company page, 'Careers' section, or in recent news articles; use Crunchbase or PitchBook if available

columns.name: recent_funding
columns.description: Most recent funding round amount and date (if applicable)
columns.validation_strategy: Search Crunchbase, PitchBook, or TechCrunch for recent funding announcements; verify with press releases on company website

columns.name: key_technologies
columns.description: Primary technologies, platforms, or research areas
columns.validation_strategy: Extract from 'Technology' or 'Research' sections on official website; review recent publications, patents, or product documentation

columns.name: official_website
columns.description: URL of the entity's primary website
columns.validation_strategy: Confirm URL via Crunchbase, LinkedIn, or reputable directory; ensure site is active and matches entity name

columns.name: source_last_updated
columns.description: Date when the information was last verified or updated
columns.validation_strategy: Record the date of data collection from each source; prioritize most recent updates across all sources

search_strategy.description: This table aims to catalog leading organizations in artificial intelligence (AI) across research, healthcare, and enterprise domains, with a focus on entities that are actively hiring, innovating, or receiving funding. Target entities must have a public presence, verifiable team size, and clear domain focus. Data will be sourced from official websites, industry directories, Crunchbase, LinkedIn, and recent news/press releases to ensure reliability and recency.

subdomains.name: AI Research Organizations
subdomains.focus: Academic labs, university-affiliated research groups, and non-profit AI institutes

subdomains.name: Healthcare AI Companies
subdomains.focus: Startups and established companies applying AI to medical diagnostics, drug discovery, and healthcare operations
search_queries: medical AI startups with recent funding

subdomains.name: Enterprise AI Providers
subdomains.focus: B2B companies offering AI-powered analytics, automation, and decision-support tools for businesses
search_queries: AI analytics platforms for business comparison

final_rows.match_rationale: Stanford Artificial Intelligence Laboratory (SAIL) is a globally recognized, university-affiliated research lab with a clear public presence, active hiring, and strong industry partnerships, directly matching all requirements[1].

Carnegie Mellon University’s School of Computer Science, including the Robotics Institute and Language Technologies Institute, is a top-tier academic AI research organization with verifiable teams, active innovation, and clear domain focus[1].

Xaira Therapeutics is a leading AI-driven drug discovery company with a $1 billion Series A in 2024, clear domain focus, and strong public presence, though detailed team size is not specified in the source.

IBM is a top-tier enterprise AI provider with a strong public presence, verifiable team size, and clear focus on B2B AI-powered analytics, automation, and decision-support tools via Watson and watsonx platforms; recent industry analysis confirms ongoing innovation and hiring, though some sourcing is from secondary industry directories.

MIT’s Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence Laboratory (CSAIL) is a leading academic AI lab with a strong public profile, though direct 2024 hiring/funding data is inferred from secondary sources.

UC Berkeley’s BAIR (Berkeley Artificial Intelligence Research) is a major university-affiliated AI research group with ongoing innovation and public presence, though detailed 2024 hiring data is from secondary sources.

Abridge is a high-profile AI ambient documentation startup with two recent mega-funding rounds ($300M Series E in June 2025, $250M Series D in February 2025), clear healthcare operations focus, and rapid adoption in health systems.
Georgia Tech’s AI-CARING Institute is an NSF-funded, university-affiliated AI research center focused on collaborative AI, with a public presence and clear domain, though the NSF map is from 2023[2].

AWS AI offers a full suite of B2B AI tools for analytics, automation, and decision support, with a strong public presence, verifiable team, and recent multi-billion-dollar investments in AI infrastructure and partnerships (e.g., Anthropic); sourcing is from reputable industry analysis.
University of Washington’s AI Institute for Dynamic Systems is an NSF-funded academic lab with a public profile and domain focus, though the NSF map is from 2023[2].
